topic: Debates in Contemporary Society (Families & Relationships / Youth / Media)
subject: sociology

items.q: Which two functions did Parsons say the family performs?
items.correct: Primary socialisation of children and the stabilisation of adult personalities.
items.misconception: Only the production of household income.
items.why: Parsons identified two 'irreducible' functions: socialising children and giving adults emotional support.

items.q: Does family diversity mean the nuclear family has disappeared?
items.correct: No - it remains common, but many other family types now exist alongside it.
items.misconception: The nuclear family no longer exists at all.
items.why: Diversity means more family forms coexist, not that the nuclear family has vanished.

items.q: What is a 'moral panic' in the media?
items.correct: An exaggerated public reaction, often amplified by the media, to a group seen as a threat.
items.misconception: A real and proportionate danger to society.
items.random_wrong: A particular type of family structure.
items.why: Cohen's concept describes media over-reaction creating 'folk devils', distorting the actual scale of an issue.

items.q: Parsons identified which two essential family functions?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Primary socialisation and stabilisation. “Reproductive and economic” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: 1990. “1970” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What was the UK fertility rate (births per woman) in 2022 according to ONS?
items.why: The real figure is 1.49 births. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
